use_cases: Automating repetitive tasks and scripting
Data analysis, machine learning, and scientific computing
Building web applications and APIs
Writing CLI tools and automation scripts

venv.description: Virtual environment to isolate project dependencies
venv.what_it_does: Creates a self-contained Python environment per project so packages don't conflict across projects
use_cases: When two projects need different versions of the same package
To keep global Python installation clean
Before starting any new Python project as good practice

pip.what_it_does: Installs, upgrades, and removes Python packages from PyPI
use_cases: Adding third-party libraries to a project
Recreating a project environment from requirements.txt
Upgrading or pinning a specific package version

list-comprehension.description: Compact way to create lists using a single line expression
list-comprehension.what_it_does: Generates a new list by applying an expression to each item in an iterable, optionally filtering
use_cases: Transforming every item in a list without a for-loop
Filtering a list based on a condition concisely
Replacing simple map() or filter() calls with readable code
examples: [x for x in range(20) if x % 2 == 0]

use_cases: Tracking changes to source code over time
Collaborating with a team on the same codebase
Creating isolated branches for features or bug fixes
Rolling back to a previous working state

commit.description: Create a snapshot of staged changes in the repository
commit.what_it_does: Records the staged changes permanently in the git history with a message describing what changed
use_cases: After completing a logical unit of work
Before switching branches to save current progress
Fixing a typo in a previous commit message with --amend

branch.what_it_does: Branches let you work on features or fixes in isolation without affecting the main codebase
use_cases: Starting work on a new feature without breaking main
Experimenting with changes safely
Cleaning up stale branches after merging
